PROOF OF SERVICE FOR MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED (TRANSACTION ID # 100049585) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.

DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED IS GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANT MARINA MIKELADZE FAILED TO RESPOND TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 1-10. THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS SPECIFIED THEREIN ARE DEEMED ADMITTED BY HER. ANY PARTY WHO CONTESTS A TENTATIVE RULING MUST SEND AN EMAIL TO AARON@MINNISANDSMALLETS.COM WITH A COPY TO ALL OTHER PARTIES BY 4PM STATING, WITHOUT ARGUMENT, THE PORTION(S) OF THE TENTATIVE RULING THAT THE PARTY CONTESTS. THE SUBJECT LINE OF THE EMAIL SHALL INCLUDE THE LINE NUMBER, CASE NAME AND CASE NUMBER. IF THE TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT CONTESTED, THE PARTIES ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE PRO TEM HEARING THE MOTION AND THE PRO TEM WILL SIGN AN ORDER CONFIRMING THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST BRING THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE HEARING EVEN IF THE MOTION IS NOT OPPOSED OR THE TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT CONTESTED. PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED; JUDGE:AARON MINNIS; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-22-2020 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-19-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is Granted. No opposition filed.

After hearing, the court signed the proposed order and judgment included with the moving papers.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2020 Set For Trial On May-26-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted
Off Calendar Sep-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: no appearance:

Granted, no opposition filed. Defendant Marina Mikeladze failed to respond to Requests for Admission Nos. 1-10. The truth of the matters specified therein are deemed admitted by her.
Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Proceedings not reported; Judge:Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Johnny Sengmany (302/JPT)
